description: do not prefer an IP version
prefer IPv4 if version is not explicitly specified
prefer IPv6 if version is not explicitly specified

name: D
description: analyze file descriptors before loop

name: L
description: try to obtain lock, or fail

name: R
description: raw dump of data flowing from right to left

name: S
description: log these signals, override default

name: T
description: total inactivity timeout in seconds

name: U
description: unidirectional mode (right to left)

name: V
description: print version and feature information to stdout, and exit

name: W
description: try to obtain lock, or wait

name: b
description: set data buffer size (8192)

name: d
description: increase verbosity (use up to 4 times; 2 are recommended)

name: d0
description: |1|2|3|4    set verbosity level (0: Errors; 4 all including Debug)

name: experimental
description: enable experimental features

name: flag-h
description: -hh like -h, plus a list of all common address option names -hhh like -hh, plus a list of all available address option names -d[ddd] increase verbosity (use up to 4 times; 2 are recommended) -d0|1|2|3|4 set verbosity level (0: Errors; 4 all including Debug) -D analyze file descriptors before loop --experimental enable experimental features --statistics output transfer statistics on exit -ly[facility] log to syslog, using facility (default is daemon) -lf<logfile> log to file -ls log to stderr (default if no other log) -lm[facility] mixed log mode (stderr during initialization, then syslog) -lp<progname> set the program name used for logging and vars -lu use microseconds for logging timestamps -lh add hostname to log messages -v verbose text dump of data traffic -x verbose hexadecimal dump of data traffic -r <file> raw dump of data flowing from left to right -R <file> raw dump of data flowing from right to left -b<size_t> set data buffer size (8192) -s sloppy (continue on error) -S<sigmask> log these signals, override default -t<timeout> wait seconds before closing second channel -T<timeout> total inactivity timeout in seconds -u unidirectional mode (left to right) -U unidirectional mode (right to left) -g do not check option groups -L <lockfile> try to obtain lock, or fail -W <lockfile> try to obtain lock, or wait -0 do not prefer an IP version -4 prefer IPv4 if version is not explicitly specified -6 prefer IPv6 if version is not explicitly specified

name: g
description: do not check option groups

name: hh
description: plus a list of all common address option names

name: hhh
description: plus a list of all available address option names

name: lf
description: log to file

name: lh
description: add hostname to log messages

name: lm
description: mixed log mode (stderr during initialization, then syslog)

name: lp
description: set the program name used for logging and vars

name: ls
description: log to stderr (default if no other log)

name: lu
description: use microseconds for logging timestamps

name: ly
description: log to syslog, using facility (default is daemon)

name: r
description: raw dump of data flowing from left to right

name: s
description: sloppy

name: t
description: wait seconds before closing second channel

name: u
description: unidirectional mode (left to right)

name: v
description: verbose text dump of data traffic

name: x
description: verbose hexadecimal dump of data traffic
